Resolution order:\n53\t        # constructor arg → ADC_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID env var → None.\n54\t        # None (default) means the push methods gracefully no-op with a WARNING\n55\t        # rather than hard-failing, preserving the pre-config behavior.\n56\t        if chat_id is not None:\n57\t            self.chat_id = chat_id\n58\t        else:\n59\t            self.chat_id = os.getenv(\"ADC_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID\") or None\n60\t        # Rate-limit window for repeated-failure DEBUG logs. Resolution order:\n61\t        # constructor arg → ADC_TELEGRAM_FAILURE_LOG_INTERVAL_SECONDS env var\n62\t        # → DEFAULT_FAILURE_LOG_INTERVAL_SECONDS. Invalid env values fall back\n63\t        # to the default rather than crashing the singleton on startup.\n64\t        interval = failure_log_interval_seconds\n65\t        if interval is None:\n66\t            env_val = os.getenv(\"ADC_TELEGRAM_FAILURE_LOG_INTERVAL_SECONDS\")\n67\t            try:\n68\t                interval = float(env_val) if env_val else self.DEFAULT_FAILURE_LOG_INTERVAL_SECONDS\n69\t            except (TypeError, ValueError):\n70\t                interval = self.DEFAULT_FAILURE_LOG_INTERVAL_SECONDS\n71\t        self._failure_log_interval_seconds: float = float(interval)\n72\t\n73\t        # Reachability — a separate logical object; its OTHER writers (send success\n74\t        # below, and the health check) are intentionally NOT under the failure lock.\n75\t        self._is_reachable = None  # None = unknown, True = reachable, False = unreachable\n76\t        # When reachability was last determined (startup probe or a reactive\n77\t        # send success/failure). Mirrors every write to _is_reachable via\n78\t        # _set_reachable(); None until the first determination. Exposed in the\n79\t        # /api/v1/status/telegram_bridge payload as ``last_check_time``.\n80\t        self._last_check_time: Optional[datetime] = None\n81\t\n82\t        # First-failure record: flat instance vars on the singleton, per-startup,\n83\t        # no persistence. Exactly one WARNING is emitted per process startup.\n84\t        self._has_logged_first_failure: bool = False\n85\t        self._failure_count: int = 0\n86\t        self._first_failure_timestamp: Optional[datetime] = None  # set-once\n87\t        self._last_failure_timestamp: Optional[datetime] = None  # updated every failure\n88\t\n89\t        # Distinct failure types already logged (WARNING'd) this startup. Drives\n90\t        # per-failure-type dedup (adc-15u0): a type NOT in this set is logged\n91\t        # immediately and independently, so a new failure type is never swallowed\n92\t        # by the ongoing-outage cooldown. Cleared by reset_first_failure_state.\n93\t        self._seen_failure_types: set[str] = set()\n94\t\n95\t        # Rate-limit / dedup window for repeated-failure logs. Failures that occur\n96\t        # inside a quiet window are counted silently; when the window elapses one\n97\t        # DEBUG summary is emitted reporting the burst size, then a new window\n98\t        # starts. `_last_repeated_log_timestamp` is seeded by the first-failure\n99\t        # WARNING so it is not immediately followed by a DEBUG storm.\n100\t        self._last_repeated_log_timestamp: Optional[datetime] = None\n101\t        self._failures_since_last_log: int = 0\n102\t\n103\t        # Serializes the first-failure claim-and-set. The critical section\n104\t        # (_record_failure_locked) is await-free on purpose so the read-then-set\n105\t        # of the flag cannot be interleaved by another coroutine.\n106\t        self._first_failure_lock: asyncio.Lock = asyncio.Lock()\n107\t\n108\t    async def send_message(\n109\t        self,\n110\t        chat_id: int | str,\n111\t        message: str,\n112\t        parse_mode: str = \"HTML\",\n113\t    ) -> bool:\n114\t        \"\"\"\n115\t        Send a message to a Telegram chat.\n116\t\n117\t        Args:\n118\t            chat_id: Telegram chat ID (int or str)\n119\t            message: Message text to send\n120\t            parse_mode: Parse mode for formatting (Markdown, HTML, etc.)\n121\t\n122\t        Returns:\n123\t            True if successful, False otherwise.\n124\t        \"\"\"\n125\t        try:\n126\t            async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:\n127\t                # telegram-claude-bridge proxy API uses /send endpoint\n128\t                # Contract: {chat_id, text, parse_mode?, thread_id?, reply_to_message_id?}\n129\t                response = await client.post(\n130\t                    f\"{self.bridge_url}/send\",\n131\t                    json={\n132\t                        \"chat_id\": int(chat_id) if isinstance(chat_id, str) else chat_id,\n133\t                        \"text\": message,\n134\t                        \"parse_mode\": parse_mode,\n135\t                    },\n136\t                    timeout=10.0,\n137\t                )\n138\t\n139\t                if response.status_code == 200:\n140\t                    logger.info(f\"Sent Telegram message to chat {chat_id}\")\n141\t                    self._set_reachable(True)  # Update reachability state\n142\t                    return True\n143\t                else:\n144\t                    error_msg = f\"status {response.status_code} - {response.text}\"\n145\t                    await self._handle_send_failure(error_context=error_msg)\n146\t                    return False\n147\t\n148\t        except httpx.RequestError as e:\n149\t            await self._handle_send_failure(error=e)\n150\t            return False\n151\t        except Exception as e:\n152\t            await self._handle_send_failure(error=e)\n153\t            return False\n154\t\n155\t    async def send_result(self, chat_id: int | str, result: dict) -> bool:\n156\t        \"\"\"\n157\t        Send a structured result to Telegram.\n158\t\n159\t        Args:\n160\t            chat_id: Telegram chat ID\n161\t            result: Result dict with keys: summary, urgency, data\n162\t\n163\t        Formats the result as a readable message and sends it.\n164\t        \"\"\"\n165\t        message = self._format_result_message(result)\n166\t        return await self.send_message(chat_id, message)\n167\t\n168\t    async def send_exception(\n169\t        self,\n170\t        session_id: str,\n171\t        exception: dict,\n172\t    ) -> bool:\n173\t        \"\"\"\n174\t        Send an exception to Telegram for human attention.\n175\t\n176\t        Routes to the single configured chat id (ADC_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID). When configured, the\n179\t        exception is formatted via ``_format_exception_message`` and delivered\n180\t        through ``send_message``, returning the bridge's real success/failure.\n181\t        \"\"\"\n182\t        if self.chat_id is None:\n183\t            logger.warning(\n184\t                f\"send_exception() called for session {session_id} - \"\n185\t                f\"no Telegram chat id configured (set ADC_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID). \"\n186\t                f\"Exception push skipped.\"\n187\t            )\n188\t            return False\n189\t\n190\t        message = self._format_exception_message(exception)\n191\t        return await self.send_message(self.chat_id, message)\n192\t\n193\t    async def send_workload_summary(\n194\t        self,\n195\t        session_id: str,\n196\t        summary: dict,\n197\t    ) -> bool:\n198\t        \"\"\"\n199\t        Send a workload summary to Telegram.\n200\t\n201\t        Routes to the single configured chat id (ADC_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID). self._format_workload_summary(summary)\n216\t        return await self.send_message(self.chat_id, message)\n217\t\n218\t    async def register_surface(self, session_id: str, telegram_chat_id: str) -> architecture where it manages sessions internally per forum topic,\n227\t        not a push-based model where external systems register delivery surfaces.\n228\t\n229\t        Returns True for check_bridge_available(self) -> bool:\n239\t        \"\"\"Check if telegram-claude-bridge is available.\"\"\"\n240\t        try:\n241\t            async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:\n242\t                response = await client.get(\n243\t                    f\"{self.bridge_url}/health\",\n244\t                    timeout=5.0,\n245\t                )\n246\t                is_available = response.status_code == 200\n247\t                self._set_reachable(is_available)\n248\t                return is_available\n249\t        except Exception:\n250\t            self._set_reachable(False)\n251\t            return False\n252\t\n253\t    def _set_reachable(self, value: bool, *, now: Optional[datetime] = None) -> None:\n254\t        \"\"\"Record a reachability determination and when it was made.\n255\t\n256\t        Centralizes every write to ``_is_reachable`` so ``last_check_time``\n257\t        always reflects the most recent determination — whether it came from\n258\t        the startup health probe (``check_bridge_available``) or a reactive\n259\t        update during ``send_message`` (success → True, failure → False).\n260\t\n261\t        Args:\n262\t            value: The reachability value to record.\n263\t            now: Optional precomputed timestamp to reuse (the failure path\n264\t                already computes one); defaults to ``datetime.now()``.\n265\t        \"\"\"\n266\t        self._is_reachable = value\n267\t        self._last_check_time = now or datetime.now()\n268\t\n269\t    def get_bridge_status(self) -> dict:\n270\t        \"\"\"\n271\t        Get the current bridge status.\n272\t\n273\t        Lock-free read: single-field atomic reads; monitoring tolerates staleness.\n274\t\n275\t        Returns:\n276\t            Dict with keys:\n277\t            - reachable: bool or None (None = unknown yet)\n278\t            - bridge_url: str\n279\t            - last_check_time: ISO-8601 string or None (when reachability was\n280\t              last determined, via the startup probe or a reactive send)\n281\t            - failure_count: int\n282\t            - has_logged_first_failure: bool\n283\t            - first_failure_timestamp: ISO-8601 string or None\n284\t            - last_failure_timestamp: ISO-8601 string or None\n285\t            - failure_log_interval_seconds: float (configured rate-limit window)\n286\t            - failures_since_last_log: int (dedup counter for the current window)\n287\t            - seen_failure_types: list[str] (distinct failure types logged this\n288\t              startup; per-type dedup — adc-15u0)\n289\t            - distinct_failure_types: int (len of seen_failure_types)\n290\t        \"\"\"\n291\t        return {\n292\t            \"reachable\": self._is_reachable,\n293\t            \"bridge_url\": self.bridge_url,\n294\t            \"chat_id\": self.chat_id,\n295\t            \"last_check_time\": self._last_check_time.isoformat()\n296\t            if self._last_check_time else None,\n297\t            \"failure_count\": self._failure_count,\n298\t            \"has_logged_first_failure\": self._has_logged_first_failure,\n299\t            \"first_failure_timestamp\": self._first_failure_timestamp.isoformat()\n300\t            if self._first_failure_timestamp else None,\n301\t            \"last_failure_timestamp\": self._last_failure_timestamp.isoformat()\n302\t            if self._last_failure_timestamp else None,\n303\t            \"failure_log_interval_seconds\": self._failure_log_interval_seconds,\n304\t            \"failures_since_last_log\": self._failures_since_last_log,\n305\t            \"seen_failure_types\": sorted(self._seen_failure_types),\n306\t            \"distinct_failure_types\": len(self._seen_failure_types),\n307\t        }\n308\t\n309\t    async def _handle_send_failure(\n310\t        self,\n311\t        error: Exception | None = None,\n312\t        error_context: str = \"\",\n313\t    ) -> None:\n314\t        \"\"\"Reactive detection entry for a Telegram send failure.\n315\t\n316\t        Called only from ``send_message``'s failure branches. Logs a WARNING that\n317\t        includes the error type and message on the FIRST failure after startup;\n318\t        every later failure in the startup is logged at DEBUG only. Exactly one\n319\t        WARNING is emitted per process startup.\n320\t\n321\t        Args:\n322\t            error: The exception that caused the failure, if any. Its type name\n323\t                and message are included in the log. ``None`` for non-2xx HTTP\n324\t                responses (httpx does not raise for those).\n325\t            error_context: Free-form context (e.g. ``\"status 500 - ...\"``) used as\n326\t                the message when no exception is available, or to enrich one.\n327\t        \"\"\"\n328\t        async with self._first_failure_lock:\n329\t            self._record_failure_locked(error=error, error_context=error_context)\n330\t\n331\t    def _repeated_log_cooldown_elapsed(self, now: datetime) -> bool:\n332\t        \"\"\"True if the rate-limit window has elapsed and a DEBUG summary may be logged.\n333\t\n334\t        Caller MUST hold ``_first_failure_lock``. Returns True immediately when no\n335\t        repeated log has been emitted yet in this startup.\n336\t        \"\"\"\n337\t        if self._last_repeated_log_timestamp is None:\n338\t            return True\n339\t        elapsed = (now - self._last_repeated_log_timestamp).total_seconds()\n340\t        return elapsed >= self._failure_log_interval_seconds\n341\t\n342\t    def _record_failure_locked(\n343\t        self,\n344\t        error: Exception | None = None,\n345\t        error_context: str = \"\",\n346\t    ) -> bool:\n347\t        \"\"\"Record a failure and emit the appropriate (rate-limited) log line.\n348\t\n349\t        Caller MUST hold ``_first_failure_lock``. Sync on purpose — no ``await``\n350\t        inside, so the read-then-set of the flags cannot be interleaved by\n351\t        another coroutine.\n352\t\n353\t        Logging policy (per-failure-type dedup, adc-15u0):\n354\t        - The FIRST failure after startup emits exactly one WARNING (the\n355\t          ``_has_logged_first_failure`` False→True claim, one per process startup)\n356\t          and seeds that failure type's dedup window.\n357\t        - A later failure of a NEW type (one not in ``_seen_failure_types``) is\n358\t          logged immediately and independently with its own WARNING, so a\n359\t          different failure type is never swallowed by the ongoing-outage\n360\t          cooldown. It also (re)seeds the rate-limit window.\n361\t        - A later failure of an already-seen type is rate-limited: at most one\n362\t          DEBUG summary per ``_failure_log_interval_seconds`` window. Failures\n363\t          inside a window are counted silently (deduped) so a sustained outage\n364\t          cannot spam the log.\n365\t\n366\t        ``_failure_count`` and ``_last_failure_timestamp`` are updated on every\n367\t        call regardless of whether anything is logged.\n368\t\n369\t        Returns:\n370\t            True iff THIS call performed the first-failure claim (the\n371\t            ``_has_logged_first_failure`` False→True flip); False otherwise.\n372\t            \"First\" is the winner of the claim, not a timestamp comparison.\n373\t        \"\"\"\n374\t        now = datetime.now()\n375\t        self._set_reachable(False, now=now)\n376\t        self._failure_count += 1\n377\t        self._last_failure_timestamp = now\n378\t        self._failures_since_last_log += 1\n379\t\n380\t        if error is not None:\n381\t            error_type = type(error).__name__\n382\t            message = str(error) or error_context or \"unknown error\"\n383\t        else:\n384\t            error_type = \"HTTPError\"\n385\t            message = error_context or \"unknown error\"\n386\t\n387\t        is_new_failure_type = error_type not in self._seen_failure_types\n388\t\n389\t        if not self._has_logged_first_failure:\n390\t            # First failure after startup — WARNING with error type + message.\n391\t            # This is the single per-startup \"umbrella\" WARNING (adc-hyqc); it\n392\t            # also records + seeds this failure type's dedup window.\n393\t            self._has_logged_first_failure = True\n394\t            self._first_failure_timestamp = now\n395\t            self._seen_failure_types.add(error_type)\n396\t            # Seed the rate-limit window so the WARNING is not immediately\n397\t            # followed by a DEBUG storm.\n398\t            self._last_repeated_log_timestamp = now\n399\t            self._failures_since_last_log = 0\n400\t            logger.warning(\n401\t                f\"First Telegram send failure detected at {self.bridge_url}. \"\n402\t                f\"Error type: {error_type}. Error: {message}. \"\n403\t                f\"Subsequent failures of the same type are rate-limited (one \"\n404\t                f\"DEBUG summary per {self._failure_log_interval_seconds:g}s); \"\n405\t                f\"a different failure type is logged independently.\"\n406\t            )\n407\t            return True\n408\t\n409\t        if is_new_failure_type:\n410\t            # A DIFFERENT failure type appeared during an ongoing outage. Log it\n411\t            # immediately and independently so it is never swallowed by the\n412\t            # same-type cooldown (adc-15u0: per-failure-type dedup). (Re)seed the\n413\t            # rate-limit window so this type's immediate repeats are deduped.\n414\t            self._seen_failure_types.add(error_type)\n415\t            self._last_repeated_log_timestamp = now\n416\t            self._failures_since_last_log = 0\n417\t            logger.warning(\n418\t                f\"New Telegram send failure type during ongoing outage at \"\n419\t                f\"{self.bridge_url}: {error_type}. Error: {message}. \"\n420\t                f\"Logged independently of the \"\n421\t                f\"{self._failure_log_interval_seconds:g}s same-type cooldown. \"\n422\t                f\"(Total failures: {self._failure_count}; distinct failure \"\n423\t                f\"types: {len(self._seen_failure_types)}.)\"\n424\t            )\n425\t            return False\n426\t\n427\t        # Repeated failure of an already-seen type — emit a DEBUG summary only\n428\t        # when the rate-limit window has elapsed; otherwise count it silently to\n429\t        # avoid log spam.\n430\t        if self._repeated_log_cooldown_elapsed(now):\n431\t            batch = self._failures_since_last_log  # failures accumulated since last log\n432\t            logger.debug(\n433\t                f\"Repeated Telegram send failures: {batch} failure(s) since last \"\n434\t                f\"log (total {self._failure_count}) at {self.bridge_url}. \"\n435\t                f\"Latest error type: {error_type}. Error: {message}.\"\n436\t            )\n437\t            self._last_repeated_log_timestamp = now\n438\t            self._failures_since_last_log = 0\n439\t\n440\t        return False\n441\t\n442\t    async def reset_first_failure_state(self) -> None:\n443\t        \"\"\"Re-arm first-failure detection.\n444\t\n445\t        Resets the claim flag, the first-failure timestamp, and the rate-limit\n446\t        window under the lock, so the next failure is treated as \"first\" again\n447\t        and starts a fresh quiet period. Used by tests and by future\n448\t        recovery-based reset hooks. The diagnostic counters\n449\t        (``_failure_count``, ``_last_failure_timestamp``) are intentionally\n450\t        retained.\n451\t        \"\"\"\n452\t        async with self._first_failure_lock:\n453\t            self._has_logged_first_failure = False\n454\t            self._first_failure_timestamp = None\n455\t            self._last_repeated_log_timestamp = None\n456\t            self._failures_since_last_log = 0\n457\t            # Re-arm per-type dedup so the next failure (even of a previously\n458\t            # seen type) is treated as a fresh first occurrence.\n459\t            self._seen_failure_types.clear()\n460\t\n461\t    def _format_result_message(self, result: dict) -> str:\n462\t        \"\"\"Format a result as a Telegram message.\"\"\"\n463\t        summary = result.get(\"summary\", \"Result available\")\n464\t        urgency = result.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\")\n465\t\n466\t        emoji_map = {\n467\t            \"critical\": \"🚨\",\n468\t            \"high\": \"⚠️\",\n469\t            \"normal\": \"📌\",\n470\t            \"low\": \"💬\",\n471\t        }\n472\t\n473\t        emoji = emoji_map.get(urgency, \"📌\")\n474\t\n475\t        lines = [\n476\t            f\"{emoji} *{summary}*\",\n477\t            \"\",\n478\t        ]\n479\t\n480\t        # Add detail from data\n481\t        data = result.get(\"data\", {})\n482\t\n483\t        if \"bead_id\" in data:\n484\t            lines.append(f\"📝 Bead: `{data['bead_id']}`\")\n485\t\n486\t        if \"title\" in data:\n487\t            lines.append(f\"📋 {data['title']}\")\n488\t\n489\t        if \"status\" in data:\n490\t            lines.append(f\"✅ Status: {data['status']}\")\n491\t\n492\t        # Add truncated body if available\n493\t        if \"body\" in data and data[\"body\"]:\n494\t            body = data[\"body\"]\n495\t            if len(body) > 300:\n496\t                body = body[:300] + \"...\"\n497\t            lines.append(\"\")\n498\t            lines.append(f\"📄 {body}\")\n499\t\n500\t        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n501\t\n502\t    def _format_exception_message(self, exception: dict) -> str:\n503\t        \"\"\"Format an exception as a prominent Telegram message.\"\"\"\n504\t        title = exception.get(\"title\", \"Attention Required\")\n505\t        urgency = exception.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\")\n506\t        context = exception.get(\"context\", \"\")\n507\t        options = exception.get(\"options\", [])\n508\t\n509\t        emoji_map = {\n510\t            \"critical\": \"🚨\",\n511\t            \"high\": \"⚠️\",\n512\t            \"normal\": \"🔔\",\n513\t        }\n514\t\n515\t        emoji = emoji_map.get(urgency, \"🔔\")\n516\t\n517\t        lines = [\n518\t            f\"{emoji} *{title}*\",\n519\t            \"\",\n520\t        ]\n521\t\n522\t        if context:\n523\t            lines.append(f\"📝 {context}\")\n524\t            lines.append(\"\")\n525\t\n526\t        if options:\n527\t            lines.append(\"🔹 Options:\")\n528\t            for i, option in enumerate(options, 1):\n529\t                lines.append(f\"  {i}. {option}\")\n530\t\n531\t        lines.append(\"\")\n532\t        lines.append(\"Reply with your choice or type a custom response.\")\n533\t\n534\t        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n535\t\n536\t    def _format_workload_summary(self, summary: dict) -> str:\n537\t        \"\"\"Format a workload summary as a Telegram message.\"\"\"\n538\t        pending = summary.get(\"pending_intents\", 0)\n539\t        new_results = summary.get(\"new_results\", 0)\n540\t        exceptions = summary.get(\"unresolved_exceptions\", 0)\n541\t\n542\t        lines = [\n543\t            \"📊 *Workload Summary*\",\n544\t            \"\",\n545\t        ]\n546\t\n547\t        if pending > 0:\n548\t            lines.append(f\"⏳ Pending intents: {pending}\")\n549\t\n550\t        if new_results > 0:\n551\t            lines.append(f\"📌 New results: {new_results}\")\n552\t\n553\t        if exceptions > 0:\n554\t            lines.append(f\"🚨 Unresolved exceptions: {exceptions}\")\n555\t\n556\t        if not any([pending, new_results, exceptions]):\n557\t            lines.append(\"✨ All caught up!\")\n558\t\n559\t        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n560\t\n561\t\n562\t# Global Telegram fallback instance\n563\t_telegram_fallback: Optional[TelegramFallback] = None\n564\t\n565\t\n566\tdef get_telegram_fallback() -> TelegramFallback:\n567\t    \"\"\"Get or create the global Telegram fallback instance.\"\"\"\n568\t    global _telegram_fallback\n569\t    if _telegram_fallback is None:\n570\t        _telegram_fallback = TelegramFallback()\n571\t    return _telegram_fallback\n572\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4ddf8807-6cb2-44e0-8211-35195f7aff2e","uuid":"46f04635-dbbd-4809-811c-eb6b28c5f8a4","timestamp":"2026-07-23T23:22:42.591Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/telegram/fallback.py","content":"\"\"\"\nTelegram fallback surface integration.\n\nIntegrates with telegram-claude-bridge to provide an always-available\nfallback surface for results and exceptions.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport logging\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Optional\n\nimport httpx\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass TelegramFallback:\n    \"\"\"\n    Telegram surface integration using telegram-claude-bridge.\n\n    telegram-claude-bridge runs on the Tailscale mesh at:\n    http://telegram-claude-bridge:8000\n\n    This module provides:\n    - Message sending to active sessions\n    - Result delivery when no other surface is available\n    - Exception push for critical/high urgency items\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # telegram-claude-bridge endpoint (Tailscale mesh)\n    # Configurable via ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL env var\n    DEFAULT_BRIDGE_URL = \"http://telegram-claude-bridge:8000\"\n    # Minimum spacing (seconds) between repeated-failure DEBUG summaries, so a\n    # sustained outage cannot spam the log. Configurable via the\n    # ADC_TELEGRAM_FAILURE_LOG_INTERVAL_SECONDS env var.\n    DEFAULT_FAILURE_LOG_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 300.0\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        bridge_url: str | None = None,\n        failure_log_interval_seconds: float | None = None,\n        chat_id: str | int | None = None,\n    ):\n        import os\n        self.bridge_url = bridge_url or os.getenv(\n            \"ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL\", self.DEFAULT_BRIDGE_URL\n        )\n        # Telegram chat ID for the single user of this personal app (plan.md\n        # Tech Stack: \"single-user app\"). There is intentionally NO multi-user\n        # session→chat mapping -- one configured chat id is all the routing\n        # exception/workload/bead-close pushes need. Resolution order:\n        # constructor arg → ADC_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID env var → None.\n        # None (default) means the push methods gracefully no-op with a WARNING\n        # rather than hard-failing, preserving the pre-config behavior.\n        if chat_id is not None:\n            self.chat_id = chat_id\n        else:\n            self.chat_id = os.getenv(\"ADC_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID\") or None\n        # Rate-limit window for repeated-failure DEBUG logs. Resolution order:\n        # constructor arg → ADC_TELEGRAM_FAILURE_LOG_INTERVAL_SECONDS env var\n        # → DEFAULT_FAILURE_LOG_INTERVAL_SECONDS. Invalid env values fall back\n        # to the default rather than crashing the singleton on startup.\n        interval = failure_log_interval_seconds\n        if interval is None:\n            env_val = os.getenv(\"ADC_TELEGRAM_FAILURE_LOG_INTERVAL_SECONDS\")\n            try:\n                interval = float(env_val) if env_val else self.DEFAULT_FAILURE_LOG_INTERVAL_SECONDS\n            except (TypeError, ValueError):\n                interval = self.DEFAULT_FAILURE_LOG_INTERVAL_SECONDS\n        self._failure_log_interval_seconds: float = float(interval)\n\n        # Reachability — a separate logical object; its OTHER writers (send success\n        # below, and the health check) are intentionally NOT under the failure lock.\n        self._is_reachable = None  # None = unknown, True = reachable, False = unreachable\n        # When reachability was last determined (startup probe or a reactive\n        # send success/failure). Mirrors every write to _is_reachable via\n        # _set_reachable(); None until the first determination. Exposed in the\n        # /api/v1/status/telegram_bridge payload as ``last_check_time``.\n        self._last_check_time: Optional[datetime] = None\n\n        # First-failure record: flat instance vars on the singleton, per-startup,\n        # no persistence. Exactly one WARNING is emitted per process startup.\n        self._has_logged_first_failure: bool = False\n        self._failure_count: int = 0\n        self._first_failure_timestamp: Optional[datetime] = None  # set-once\n        self._last_failure_timestamp: Optional[datetime] = None  # updated every failure\n\n        # Distinct failure types already logged (WARNING'd) this startup. Drives\n        # per-failure-type dedup (adc-15u0): a type NOT in this set is logged\n        # immediately and independently, so a new failure type is never swallowed\n        # by the ongoing-outage cooldown. Cleared by reset_first_failure_state.\n        self._seen_failure_types: set[str] = set()\n\n        # Rate-limit / dedup window for repeated-failure logs. Failures that occur\n        # inside a quiet window are counted silently; when the window elapses one\n        # DEBUG summary is emitted reporting the burst size, then a new window\n        # starts. `_last_repeated_log_timestamp` is seeded by the first-failure\n        # WARNING so it is not immediately followed by a DEBUG storm.\n        self._last_repeated_log_timestamp: Optional[datetime] = None\n        self._failures_since_last_log: int = 0\n\n        # Serializes the first-failure claim-and-set. The critical section\n        # (_record_failure_locked) is await-free on purpose so the read-then-set\n        # of the flag cannot be interleaved by another coroutine.\n        self._first_failure_lock: asyncio.Lock = asyncio.Lock()\n\n    async def send_message(\n        self,\n        chat_id: int | str,\n        message: str,\n        parse_mode: str = \"HTML\",\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Send a message to a Telegram chat.\n\n        Args:\n            chat_id: Telegram chat ID (int or str)\n            message: Message text to send\n            parse_mode: Parse mode for formatting (Markdown, HTML, etc.)\n\n        Returns:\n            True if successful, False otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:\n                # telegram-claude-bridge proxy API uses /send endpoint\n                # Contract: {chat_id, text, parse_mode?, thread_id?, reply_to_message_id?}\n                response = await client.post(\n                    f\"{self.bridge_url}/send\",\n                    json={\n                        \"chat_id\": int(chat_id) if isinstance(chat_id, str) else chat_id,\n                        \"text\": message,\n                        \"parse_mode\": parse_mode,\n                    },\n                    timeout=10.0,\n                )\n\n                if response.status_code == 200:\n                    logger.info(f\"Sent Telegram message to chat {chat_id}\")\n                    self._set_reachable(True)  # Update reachability state\n                    return True\n                else:\n                    error_msg = f\"status {response.status_code} - {response.text}\"\n                    await self._handle_send_failure(error_context=error_msg)\n                    return False\n\n        except httpx.RequestError as e:\n            await self._handle_send_failure(error=e)\n            return False\n        except Exception as e:\n            await self._handle_send_failure(error=e)\n            return False\n\n    async def send_result(self, chat_id: int | str, result: dict) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Send a structured result to Telegram.\n\n        Args:\n            chat_id: Telegram chat ID\n            result: Result dict with keys: summary, urgency, data\n\n        Formats the result as a readable message and sends it.\n        \"\"\"\n        message = self._format_result_message(result)\n        return await self.send_message(chat_id, message)\n\n    async def send_exception(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        exception: dict,\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Send an exception to Telegram for human attention.\n\n        Routes to the single configured chat id (ADC_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID). When no\n        chat id is configured this is a graceful no-op: a WARNING is logged and\n        False is returned, matching the pre-config behavior. When configured, the\n        exception is formatted via ``_format_exception_message`` and delivered\n        through ``send_message``, returning the bridge's real success/failure.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.chat_id is None:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"send_exception() called for session {session_id} - \"\n                f\"no Telegram chat id configured (set ADC_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID). \"\n                f\"Exception push skipped.\"\n            )\n            return False\n\n        message = self._format_exception_message(exception)\n        return await self.send_message(self.chat_id, message)\n\n    async def send_workload_summary(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        summary: dict,\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Send a workload summary to Telegram.\n\n        Routes to the single configured chat id (ADC_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID). When no\n        chat id is configured this is a graceful no-op: a WARNING is logged and\n        False is returned, matching the pre-config behavior. When configured, the\n        summary is formatted via ``_format_workload_summary`` and delivered\n        through ``send_message``, returning the bridge's real success/failure.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.chat_id is None:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"send_workload_summary() called for session {session_id} - \"\n                f\"no Telegram chat id configured (set ADC_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID). \"\n                f\"Workload summary push skipped.\"\n            )\n            return False\n\n        message = self._format_workload_summary(summary)\n        return await self.send_message(self.chat_id, message)\n\n    async def register_surface(self, session_id: str, telegram_chat_id: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Register a Telegram surface for a session.\n\n        Called when a Telegram user starts a conversation.\n\n        NOTE: The /register_surface endpoint does NOT exist in telegram-claude-bridge.\n        This method is a no-op stub for API compatibility. telegram-claude-bridge\n        uses a pull-based architecture where it manages sessions internally per forum topic,\n        not a push-based model where external systems register delivery surfaces.\n\n        Returns True for compatibility (pretends registration succeeded).\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.warning(\n            f\"register_surface() called for session {session_id} - \"\n            f\"telegram-claude-bridge does not support surface registration. \"\n            f\"This is a no-op stub.\"\n        )\n        return True\n\n    async def check_bridge_available(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if telegram-claude-bridge is available.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:\n                response = await client.get(\n                    f\"{self.bridge_url}/health\",\n                    timeout=5.0,\n                )\n                is_available = response.status_code == 200\n                self._set_reachable(is_available)\n                return is_available\n        except Exception:\n            self._set_reachable(False)\n            return False\n\n    def _set_reachable(self, value: bool, *, now: Optional[datetime] = None) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Record a reachability determination and when it was made.\n\n        Centralizes every write to ``_is_reachable`` so ``last_check_time``\n        always reflects the most recent determination — whether it came from\n        the startup health probe (``check_bridge_available``) or a reactive\n        update during ``send_message`` (success → True, failure → False).\n\n        Args:\n            value: The reachability value to record.\n            now: Optional precomputed timestamp to reuse (the failure path\n                already computes one); defaults to ``datetime.now()``.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._is_reachable = value\n        self._last_check_time = now or datetime.now()\n\n    def get_bridge_status(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get the current bridge status.\n\n        Lock-free read: single-field atomic reads; monitoring tolerates staleness.\n\n        Returns:\n            Dict with keys:\n            - reachable: bool or None (None = unknown yet)\n            - bridge_url: str\n            - last_check_time: ISO-8601 string or None (when reachability was\n              last determined, via the startup probe or a reactive send)\n            - failure_count: int\n            - has_logged_first_failure: bool\n            - first_failure_timestamp: ISO-8601 string or None\n            - last_failure_timestamp: ISO-8601 string or None\n            - failure_log_interval_seconds: float (configured rate-limit window)\n            - failures_since_last_log: int (dedup counter for the current window)\n            - seen_failure_types: list[str] (distinct failure types logged this\n              startup; per-type dedup — adc-15u0)\n            - distinct_failure_types: int (len of seen_failure_types)\n        \"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"reachable\": self._is_reachable,\n            \"bridge_url\": self.bridge_url,\n            \"chat_id\": self.chat_id,\n            \"last_check_time\": self._last_check_time.isoformat()\n            if self._last_check_time else None,\n            \"failure_count\": self._failure_count,\n            \"has_logged_first_failure\": self._has_logged_first_failure,\n            \"first_failure_timestamp\": self._first_failure_timestamp.isoformat()\n            if self._first_failure_timestamp else None,\n            \"last_failure_timestamp\": self._last_failure_timestamp.isoformat()\n            if self._last_failure_timestamp else None,\n            \"failure_log_interval_seconds\": self._failure_log_interval_seconds,\n            \"failures_since_last_log\": self._failures_since_last_log,\n            \"seen_failure_types\": sorted(self._seen_failure_types),\n            \"distinct_failure_types\": len(self._seen_failure_types),\n        }\n\n    async def _handle_send_failure(\n        self,\n        error: Exception | None = None,\n        error_context: str = \"\",\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Reactive detection entry for a Telegram send failure.\n\n        Called only from ``send_message``'s failure branches. Logs a WARNING that\n        includes the error type and message on the FIRST failure after startup;\n        every later failure in the startup is logged at DEBUG only. Exactly one\n        WARNING is emitted per process startup.\n\n        Args:\n            error: The exception that caused the failure, if any. Its type name\n                and message are included in the log. ``None`` for non-2xx HTTP\n                responses (httpx does not raise for those).\n            error_context: Free-form context (e.g. ``\"status 500 - ...\"``) used as\n                the message when no exception is available, or to enrich one.\n        \"\"\"\n        async with self._first_failure_lock:\n            self._record_failure_locked(error=error, error_context=error_context)\n\n    def _repeated_log_cooldown_elapsed(self, now: datetime) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"True if the rate-limit window has elapsed and a DEBUG summary may be logged.\n\n        Caller MUST hold ``_first_failure_lock``. Returns True immediately when no\n        repeated log has been emitted yet in this startup.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self._last_repeated_log_timestamp is None:\n            return True\n        elapsed = (now - self._last_repeated_log_timestamp).total_seconds()\n        return elapsed >= self._failure_log_interval_seconds\n\n    def _record_failure_locked(\n        self,\n        error: Exception | None = None,\n        error_context: str = \"\",\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Record a failure and emit the appropriate (rate-limited) log line.\n\n        Caller MUST hold ``_first_failure_lock``. Sync on purpose — no ``await``\n        inside, so the read-then-set of the flags cannot be interleaved by\n        another coroutine.\n\n        Logging policy (per-failure-type dedup, adc-15u0):\n        - The FIRST failure after startup emits exactly one WARNING (the\n          ``_has_logged_first_failure`` False→True claim, one per process startup)\n          and seeds that failure type's dedup window.\n        - A later failure of a NEW type (one not in ``_seen_failure_types``) is\n          logged immediately and independently with its own WARNING, so a\n          different failure type is never swallowed by the ongoing-outage\n          cooldown. It also (re)seeds the rate-limit window.\n        - A later failure of an already-seen type is rate-limited: at most one\n          DEBUG summary per ``_failure_log_interval_seconds`` window. Failures\n          inside a window are counted silently (deduped) so a sustained outage\n          cannot spam the log.\n\n        ``_failure_count`` and ``_last_failure_timestamp`` are updated on every\n        call regardless of whether anything is logged.\n\n        Returns:\n            True iff THIS call performed the first-failure claim (the\n            ``_has_logged_first_failure`` False→True flip); False otherwise.\n            \"First\" is the winner of the claim, not a timestamp comparison.\n        \"\"\"\n        now = datetime.now()\n        self._set_reachable(False, now=now)\n        self._failure_count += 1\n        self._last_failure_timestamp = now\n        self._failures_since_last_log += 1\n\n        if error is not None:\n            error_type = type(error).__name__\n            message = str(error) or error_context or \"unknown error\"\n        else:\n            error_type = \"HTTPError\"\n            message = error_context or \"unknown error\"\n\n        is_new_failure_type = error_type not in self._seen_failure_types\n\n        if not self._has_logged_first_failure:\n            # First failure after startup — WARNING with error type + message.\n            # This is the single per-startup \"umbrella\" WARNING (adc-hyqc); it\n            # also records + seeds this failure type's dedup window.\n            self._has_logged_first_failure = True\n            self._first_failure_timestamp = now\n            self._seen_failure_types.add(error_type)\n            # Seed the rate-limit window so the WARNING is not immediately\n            # followed by a DEBUG storm.\n            self._last_repeated_log_timestamp = now\n            self._failures_since_last_log = 0\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"First Telegram send failure detected at {self.bridge_url}. \"\n                f\"Error type: {error_type}. Error: {message}. \"\n                f\"Subsequent failures of the same type are rate-limited (one \"\n                f\"DEBUG summary per {self._failure_log_interval_seconds:g}s); \"\n                f\"a different failure type is logged independently.\"\n            )\n            return True\n\n        if is_new_failure_type:\n            # A DIFFERENT failure type appeared during an ongoing outage. Log it\n            # immediately and independently so it is never swallowed by the\n            # same-type cooldown (adc-15u0: per-failure-type dedup). (Re)seed the\n            # rate-limit window so this type's immediate repeats are deduped.\n            self._seen_failure_types.add(error_type)\n            self._last_repeated_log_timestamp = now\n            self._failures_since_last_log = 0\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"New Telegram send failure type during ongoing outage at \"\n                f\"{self.bridge_url}: {error_type}. Error: {message}. \"\n                f\"Logged independently of the \"\n                f\"{self._failure_log_interval_seconds:g}s same-type cooldown. \"\n                f\"(Total failures: {self._failure_count}; distinct failure \"\n                f\"types: {len(self._seen_failure_types)}.)\"\n            )\n            return False\n\n        # Repeated failure of an already-seen type — emit a DEBUG summary only\n        # when the rate-limit window has elapsed; otherwise count it silently to\n        # avoid log spam.\n        if self._repeated_log_cooldown_elapsed(now):\n            batch = self._failures_since_last_log  # failures accumulated since last log\n            logger.debug(\n                f\"Repeated Telegram send failures: {batch} failure(s) since last \"\n                f\"log (total {self._failure_count}) at {self.bridge_url}. \"\n                f\"Latest error type: {error_type}. Error: {message}.\"\n            )\n            self._last_repeated_log_timestamp = now\n            self._failures_since_last_log = 0\n\n        return False\n\n    async def reset_first_failure_state(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Re-arm first-failure detection.\n\n        Resets the claim flag, the first-failure timestamp, and the rate-limit\n        window under the lock, so the next failure is treated as \"first\" again\n        and starts a fresh quiet period. Used by tests and by future\n        recovery-based reset hooks. The diagnostic counters\n        (``_failure_count``, ``_last_failure_timestamp``) are intentionally\n        retained.\n        \"\"\"\n        async with self._first_failure_lock:\n            self._has_logged_first_failure = False\n            self._first_failure_timestamp = None\n            self._last_repeated_log_timestamp = None\n            self._failures_since_last_log = 0\n            # Re-arm per-type dedup so the next failure (even of a previously\n            # seen type) is treated as a fresh first occurrence.\n            self._seen_failure_types.clear()\n\n    def _format_result_message(self, result: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Format a result as a Telegram message.\"\"\"\n        summary = result.get(\"summary\", \"Result available\")\n        urgency = result.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\")\n\n        emoji_map = {\n            \"critical\": \"🚨\",\n            \"high\": \"⚠️\",\n            \"normal\": \"📌\",\n            \"low\": \"💬\",\n        }\n\n        emoji = emoji_map.get(urgency, \"📌\")\n\n        lines = [\n            f\"{emoji} *{summary}*\",\n            \"\",\n        ]\n\n        # Add detail from data\n        data = result.get(\"data\", {})\n\n        if \"bead_id\" in data:\n            lines.append(f\"📝 Bead: `{data['bead_id']}`\")\n\n        if \"title\" in data:\n            lines.append(f\"📋 {data['title']}\")\n\n        if \"status\" in data:\n            lines.append(f\"✅ Status: {data['status']}\")\n\n        # Add truncated body if available\n        if \"body\" in data and data[\"body\"]:\n            body = data[\"body\"]\n            if len(body) > 300:\n                body = body[:300] + \"...\"\n            lines.append(\"\")\n            lines.append(f\"📄 {body}\")\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n    def _format_exception_message(self, exception: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Format an exception as a prominent Telegram message.\"\"\"\n        title = exception.get(\"title\", \"Attention Required\")\n        urgency = exception.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\")\n        context = exception.get(\"context\", \"\")\n        options = exception.get(\"options\", [])\n\n        emoji_map = {\n            \"critical\": \"🚨\",\n            \"high\": \"⚠️\",\n            \"normal\": \"🔔\",\n        }\n\n        emoji = emoji_map.get(urgency, \"🔔\")\n\n        lines = [\n            f\"{emoji} *{title}*\",\n            \"\",\n        ]\n\n        if context:\n            lines.append(f\"📝 {context}\")\n            lines.append(\"\")\n\n        if options:\n            lines.append(\"🔹 Options:\")\n            for i, option in enumerate(options, 1):\n                lines.append(f\"  {i}. {option}\")\n\n        lines.append(\"\")\n        lines.append(\"Reply with your choice or type a custom response.\")\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n    def _format_workload_summary(self, summary: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Format a workload summary as a Telegram message.\"\"\"\n        pending = summary.get(\"pending_intents\", 0)\n        new_results = summary.get(\"new_results\", 0)\n        exceptions = summary.get(\"unresolved_exceptions\", 0)\n\n        lines = [\n            \"📊 *Workload Summary*\",\n            \"\",\n        ]\n\n        if pending > 0:\n            lines.append(f\"⏳ Pending intents: {pending}\")\n\n        if new_results > 0:\n            lines.append(f\"📌 New results: {new_results}\")\n\n        if exceptions > 0:\n            lines.append(f\"🚨 Unresolved exceptions: {exceptions}\")\n\n        if not any([pending, new_results, exceptions]):\n            lines.append(\"✨ All caught up!\")\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\n# Global Telegram fallback instance\n_telegram_fallback: Optional[TelegramFallback] = None\n\n\ndef get_telegram_fallback() -> TelegramFallback:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global Telegram fallback instance.\"\"\"\n    global _telegram_fallback\n    if _telegram_fallback is None:\n        _telegram_fallback = TelegramFallback()\n    return _telegram_fallback\n","numLines":572,"startLine":1,"totalLines":572}}}

This module provides:\n    - Audio file transcription\n    - Fallback for browsers without Web Speech API\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # whisper-stt service URL (Tailscale mesh)\n    # Configurable via ADC_WHISPER_STT_URL env var\n    DEFAULT_STT_URL = \"https://whisper.ardenone.com\"\n\n    def __init__(self, stt_url: str | None = None):\n        self.stt_url = stt_url or os.getenv(\"ADC_WHISPER_STT_URL\", self.DEFAULT_STT_URL)\n        self._is_available = None  # None = unknown, True = available, False = unavailable\n        self._failure_count = 0\n\n    async def transcribe(\n        self,\n        audio_data: bytes,\n        audio_format: str = \"webm\",\n    ) -> Optional[str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Transcribe audio data to text using whisper-stt.\n\n        Args:\n            audio_data: Raw audio bytes (webm/opus format from MediaRecorder)\n            audio_format: Audio format hint (default: \"webm\")\n\n        Returns:\n            Transcribed text, or None if transcription failed.\n\n        Raises:\n            httpx.RequestError: If the request fails\n            httpx.HTTPStatusError: If the request returns an error status\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Prepare multipart form data\n            files = {\n                \"file\": (\"audio.webm\", audio_data, \"audio/webm\"),\n            }\n            data = {\n                \"format\": audio_format,\n            }\n\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30.0) as client:\n                # whisper-stt uses /transcribe endpoint\n                response = await client.post(\n                    f\"{self.stt_url}/transcribe\",\n                    files=files,\n                    data=data,\n                )\n\n                if response.status_code == 200:\n                    result = response.json()\n                    text = result.get(\"text\", \"\").strip()\n\n                    if text:\n                        logger.info(f\"STT transcription successful: {text[:50]}...\")\n                        self._is_available = True\n                        return text\n                    else:\n                        logger.warning(\"STT returned empty transcription\")\n                        self._is_available = False\n                        return None\n                else:\n                    error_msg = f\"status {response.status_code} - {response.text}\"\n                    self._handle_transcription_failure(error_msg)\n                    return None\n\n        except httpx.RequestError as e:\n            error_msg = f\"request error: {e}\"\n            self._handle_transcription_failure(error_msg)\n            return None\n        except Exception as e:\n            error_msg = f\"unexpected error: {e}\"\n            self._handle_transcription_failure(error_msg)\n            return None\n\n    async def check_available(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if whisper-stt service is available.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5.0) as client:\n                # Try to reach the health endpoint\n                response = await client.get(\n                    f\"{self.stt_url}/health\",\n                )\n                is_available = response.status_code == 200\n                self._is_available = is_available\n                return is_available\n        except Exception:\n            self._is_available = False\n            return False\n\n    def get_status(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get the current STT service status.\n\n        Returns:\n            Dict with keys:\n            - available: bool or None (None = unknown yet)\n            - stt_url: str\n            - failure_count: int\n        \"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"available\": self._is_available,\n            \"stt_url\": self.stt_url,\n            \"failure_count\": self._failure_count,\n        }\n\n    def _handle_transcription_failure(self, error_context: str = \"\"):\n        \"\"\"Handle a transcription failure.\"\"\"\n        self._is_available = False\n        self._failure_count += 1\n        logger.warning(\n            f\"STT transcription failed at {self.stt_url}. \"\n            f\"Error: {error_context if error_context else 'unknown error'}. \"\n            f\"Failure #{self._failure_count}.\"\n        )\n\n\n# Global STT fallback instance\n_stt_fallback: Optional[STTFallback] = None\n\n\ndef get_stt_fallback() -> STTFallback:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global STT fallback instance.\"\"\"\n    global _stt_fallback\n    if _stt_fallback is None:\n        _stt_fallback = STTFallback()\n    return _stt_fallback\n","numLines":151,"startLine":1,"totalLines":151}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_00b683a90e074ccc96ccdab8","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# aide-de-camp: Application Plan\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\taide-de-camp is a universal personal interface that accepts stream-of-consciousness voice and text input, routes it to parallel agents across any domain, and renders results as a live canvas of HTML cards or as synthesized audio narration. It is built on top of existing personal infrastructure and improves itself through conversational feedback after initial bootstrap.\n6\t\n7\tCLI invocation: `adc`\n8\t\n9\t---\n10\t\n11\t## Problem Statement\n12\t\n13\tManaging multiple active projects requires constant context-switching between dedicated tabs, terminals, and chat sessions. The input channel (typing into a specific Claude Code tab) is also the routing layer, and that conflation adds cognitive overhead. The same problem extends beyond coding: research, lookups, personal context, reminders — anything that requires knowing which system to ask before you can ask it.\n14\t\n15\taide-de-camp eliminates that overhead by providing a single input surface that routes automatically, dispatches in parallel, and returns organized results without requiring the user to know where to ask.\n16\t\n17\t---\n18\t\n19\t## Core Architecture\n20\t\n21\t### The Hot Path\n22\t\n23\t```\n24\tUser utterance (voice or text)\n25\t  → STT transcription (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)\n26\t  → Intent Router (one LLM call, haiku-class, ~500ms est.)\n27\t      output: [{project_slug, intent_type, urgency, utterance_fragment,\n28\t                lookup_kind (lookup intents only)}, ...]\n29\t  → N parallel Fetch+Synthesize workers (one per intent thread)\n30\t      Fetch: deterministic code, executes command matrix based on intent_type\n31\t             (lookup intents: intent_type + lookup_kind)\n32\t      Synthesize: one LLM call per thread (sonnet-class, ~1-2s est.)\n33\t      output: {data, summary} — urgency carries through unchanged from the\n34\t              intent thread (see Urgency ownership)\n35\t  → Server-side card render (no LLM): deterministic component lookup via\n36\t      component_usage_patterns, template filled into card_cache;\n37\t      no match → result flagged for the built-in fallback card\n38\t  → Cards streamed via SSE to active canvas — client injects the rendered\n39\t      HTML, or renders fallback-flagged results with its built-in generic card\n40\t```\n41\t\n42\tTarget: < 3 seconds from utterance to first partial result on canvas. No Claude Code session startup on this path.\n43\t\n44\t> The ~500ms and ~1-2s figures above — and every per-stage number in this plan — are design **estimates**. Nothing on the hot path has been measured yet. See Latency Budget & Instrumentation.\n45\t\n46\t### Latency Budget & Instrumentation\n47\t\n48\tThe <3s promise is the product's central claim, so it gets a budget, instrumentation, and a gate — not just a diagram annotation. Per-stage targets below are estimates chosen so the end-to-end budget is plausible; the Measured columns show actual captured timings from 206 production-like runs across three test shapes (see bead adc-2xf52 for detailed analysis).\n49\t\n50\t| Stage | Target (ESTIMATE) | Measured p50 | Measured p95 |\n51\t|-------|-------------------|--------------|--------------|\n52\t| STT final transcript (Web Speech API) | ~300ms | *Not measured* (client-side) | *Not measured* (client-side) |\n53\t| Intent Router (haiku-class via ZAI proxy) | ~500ms | **1,587-2,074ms** ❌ | **2,527-4,301ms** ❌ |\n54\t| Fetch — first source returns (surfaces as a per-source progress state on the pending card) | ~500ms | **0-16ms** ✅ | **0-21ms** ✅ |\n55\t| Fetch — window closes (all sources resolved or timed out; gates synthesize start — see Fetch Strand) | ~1s | **0-51ms** ✅ | **0-191ms** ✅ |\n56\t| Synthesize — first token (sonnet-class via ZAI proxy; starts at fetch-window close) | ~1s (cap set by the e2e gate — see internal-consistency note) | *Not measured* (instrumentation gap) | *Not measured* (instrumentation gap) |\n57\t| Synthesize — total (first token through completion) | ~1-2s | **3,108-3,984ms** ❌ | **4,663-7,877ms** ❌ |\n58\t| SSE emit → first card render | ~100ms | *Not measured* | *Not measured* |\n59\t| Escalate — bead formulation + safety validation + `bf create` (haiku-class via ZAI proxy; off the first-card path, see note below) | ~2s | **3,992ms** ❌ | **5,445ms** ❌ |\n60\t| **End-to-end: utterance end → first partial card** | **< 3s** | **5,219-5,571ms** ❌ | **8,853-10,404ms** ❌ |\n61\t\n62\t**Internal consistency — the e2e row is the binding gate.** The hot-path stages are strictly sequential (STT → router → fetch-window close → synthesize first token → SSE emit), so their targets must sum inside the end-to-end budget: 300 + 500 + 1000 + 1000 + 100 ms ≈ 2.9s against the 3s gate, leaving ~100ms of unallocated slack. (The fetch first-source and escalate rows sit off this critical path — the former lands inside the fetch window, the latter is off the first-card path per the note below.) Synthesize-first-token was originally sketched at ~1–2s — the Hot Path diagram's ~1–2s figure survives as the estimate for the *full* synthesize call, first token through completion — but at a 2s first token the stages sum to ~3.9s: every stage could pass its own budget individually while the end-to-end gate fails, a contradiction that would otherwise surface only at rehearsal. Hence the ~1s first-token allocation above. All per-stage targets remain ESTIMATES and are **provisional allocations, not independent pass/fail gates**: once the Measured p50/p95 columns fill, re-derive the stage allocations from measured slack (a stage running under budget donates headroom to one running over) with the e2e row held fixed and overriding — the Gate below then applies against the re-derived allocations. A run where every stage meets its allocation but the e2e row misses is still a failed run.\n63\t\n64\t**Task-profile first card.** The escalate stage does not gate the <3s promise: for a task-profile thread, the ack/pending card (a served built-in template, not a component-library render — see Component Library → Built-in cards) renders as soon as the router resolves the thread (router + SSE emit only) — before bead formulation and `bf create` complete — then updates in place with the bead ref when the escalate stage returns. The escalate row above budgets that in-place update, not the first card; the end-to-end row applies to the ack card.\n65\t\n66\t**Instrumentation requirement.** The server records per-stage timings for every dispatch — `router_ms`, `fetch_first_source_ms`, `fetch_total_ms` (fetch-window close), `synthesize_first_token_ms`, `synthesize_total_ms`, `escalate_ms` (task-profile dispatches: formulation + validation + `bf create`), `sse_emit_ms`, plus client-reported STT and first-render timestamps when available — and persists them to the session store's `dispatch_timings` table (schema in Data Model). This is not optional telemetry: it is the only way the Measured columns get filled, and it doubles as the per-step timing log the Phase 5 rehearsal checklist requires.\n67\t\n68\t**Gate.** The demo cannot be scheduled until the Measured p50/p95 columns are filled from real runs (rehearsal timing logs count). If measured p95 blows a stage's budget, either the stage gets fixed or the on-screen promise changes — the recording must not showcase a number the system doesn't hit.\n69\t\n70\t### The Async Path\n71\t\n72\t```\n73\tTask-profile intent (research, coding, long-running work)\n74\t  → Generated-Bead Safety validation + approval (see Escalate Strand)\n75\t  → NEEDLE task bead created (bf create)\n76\t  → Existing Claude Code workers pick up bead normally\n77\t  → Bead watcher detects closure event\n78\t  → Result written to session store results table\n79\t  → SSE push to active canvas (or Telegram if no canvas)\n80\t```\n81\t\n82\t> ⚠ The \"or Telegram if no canvas\" leg is NON-FUNCTIONAL today — Telegram delivery has never worked in any deployment (stubbed methods, unreachable bridge URL; see ADR-1). Until ADR-1 lands, a result arriving with no active canvas is persisted to the session store but reaches no surface.\n83\t\n84\tThe diagram above is the happy path only. Beads also get refused, fail repeatedly, or sit unclaimed — and NEEDLE has no built-in circuit breaker: it re-dispatches a failing bead forever. The async path therefore also specifies:\n85\t\n86\t- **Re-dispatch circuit breaker.** NEEDLE exposes no dispatch-attempt API, so the breaker's signal is the bead's own comment stream: a worker that declines a bead appends a structured refusal comment — `bf comment <id> \"REFUSED: <reason>\"` — the same convention the live incident's workers followed when they committed refusal records to the bead. Each tick the watcher checks its open tracked beads (every unresolved `intents.bead_ref`) alongside the closed list, parses comments past a per-bead high-water mark, and persists the running refusal count in the watcher-owned `bead_watch` table (see Data Model) — on disk, so breaker state survives watcher restarts. After N refusals (default 3) or T hours without progress (default 24h — no closure and no new non-refusal comment), it fences the bead — `bf update` to `status=blocked`, so workers stop picking it up — sets the intent's status to `stuck`, and pushes a \"task stuck — needs your input\" card to the active surface with the most recent refusal reason attached.\n87\t- **Terminal failures.** A bead closed as failed/refused resolves its intent to `failed`; the reason surfaces as a card, never silently dropped. User cancellation still maps to `cancelled`.\n88\t- **Visible aging.** Pending cards show elapsed time; a card past its SLA is flagged on canvas before the breaker trips, so a stuck task is never invisible. The SLA is defined, not vibes: built-in per-intent-type defaults (task-profile: 6h; hot-path intents flag at 30s pending, since their budget is 3s) with a per-project `sla_hours` override in the registry entry. Flag ownership is split by path: for bead-backed (task-profile) intents the watcher computes `sla_deadline` at bead creation, stores it in `bead_watch`, and stamps `sla_flagged_at` when the flag fires. Hot-path intents have no bead and no watcher row — their 30s flag is owned by the **canvas client**: a pure client-side timer from the local pending placeholder's creation at submit time (the canvas creates the placeholder before any server response — see Escalate Strand, Pending/ack card render path) applies the aged-pending treatment with no server round-trip, deliberately, because a hung or wedged server is exactly the failure this flag must survive (see the matching Degraded-State UX row).\n89\t\n90\t### The Self-Improvement Path\n91\t\n92\t```\n93\tFeedback signal (explicit instruction or implicit engagement pattern)\n94\t  → Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE task bead)\n95\t      reads target artifact (prompt file, registry YAML)\n96\t      generates update\n97\t      surfaces diff to user\n98\t  → User approves → artifact written → hot-reloaded on next invocation\n99\t```\n100\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4ddf8807-6cb2-44e0-8211-35195f7aff2e","uuid":"fcf00135-6d1e-4a24-8891-f116278a99c2","timestamp":"2026-07-23T23:24:29.133Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/docs/plan/plan.md","content":"# aide-de-camp: Application Plan\n\n## Overview\n\naide-de-camp is a universal personal interface that accepts stream-of-consciousness voice and text input, routes it to parallel agents across any domain, and renders results as a live canvas of HTML cards or as synthesized audio narration. It is built on top of existing personal infrastructure and improves itself through conversational feedback after initial bootstrap.\n\nCLI invocation: `adc`\n\n---\n\n## Problem Statement\n\nManaging multiple active projects requires constant context-switching between dedicated tabs, terminals, and chat sessions. The input channel (typing into a specific Claude Code tab) is also the routing layer, and that conflation adds cognitive overhead. The same problem extends beyond coding: research, lookups, personal context, reminders — anything that requires knowing which system to ask before you can ask it.\n\naide-de-camp eliminates that overhead by providing a single input surface that routes automatically, dispatches in parallel, and returns organized results without requiring the user to know where to ask.\n\n---\n\n## Core Architecture\n\n### The Hot Path\n\n```\nUser utterance (voice or text)\n  → STT transcription (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)\n  → Intent Router (one LLM call, haiku-class, ~500ms est.)\n      output: [{project_slug, intent_type, urgency, utterance_fragment,\n                lookup_kind (lookup intents only)}, ...]\n  → N parallel Fetch+Synthesize workers (one per intent thread)\n      Fetch: deterministic code, executes command matrix based on intent_type\n             (lookup intents: intent_type + lookup_kind)\n      Synthesize: one LLM call per thread (sonnet-class, ~1-2s est.)\n      output: {data, summary} — urgency carries through unchanged from the\n              intent thread (see Urgency ownership)\n  → Server-side card render (no LLM): deterministic component lookup via\n      component_usage_patterns, template filled into card_cache;\n      no match → result flagged for the built-in fallback card\n  → Cards streamed via SSE to active canvas — client injects the rendered\n      HTML, or renders fallback-flagged results with its built-in generic card\n```\n\nTarget: < 3 seconds from utterance to first partial result on canvas. No Claude Code session startup on this path.\n\n> The ~500ms and ~1-2s figures above — and every per-stage number in this plan — are design **estimates**. Nothing on the hot path has been measured yet. See Latency Budget & Instrumentation.\n\n### Latency Budget & Instrumentation\n\nThe <3s promise is the product's central claim, so it gets a budget, instrumentation, and a gate — not just a diagram annotation. Per-stage targets below are estimates chosen so the end-to-end budget is plausible; the Measured columns show actual captured timings from 206 production-like runs across three test shapes (see bead adc-2xf52 for detailed analysis).\n\n| Stage | Target (ESTIMATE) | Measured p50 | Measured p95 |\n|-------|-------------------|--------------|--------------|\n| STT final transcript (Web Speech API) | ~300ms | *Not measured* (client-side) | *Not measured* (client-side) |\n| Intent Router (haiku-class via ZAI proxy) | ~500ms | **1,587-2,074ms** ❌ | **2,527-4,301ms** ❌ |\n| Fetch — first source returns (surfaces as a per-source progress state on the pending card) | ~500ms | **0-16ms** ✅ | **0-21ms** ✅ |\n| Fetch — window closes (all sources resolved or timed out; gates synthesize start — see Fetch Strand) | ~1s | **0-51ms** ✅ | **0-191ms** ✅ |\n| Synthesize — first token (sonnet-class via ZAI proxy; starts at fetch-window close) | ~1s (cap set by the e2e gate — see internal-consistency note) | *Not measured* (instrumentation gap) | *Not measured* (instrumentation gap) |\n| Synthesize — total (first token through completion) | ~1-2s | **3,108-3,984ms** ❌ | **4,663-7,877ms** ❌ |\n| SSE emit → first card render | ~100ms | *Not measured* | *Not measured* |\n| Escalate — bead formulation + safety validation + `bf create` (haiku-class via ZAI proxy; off the first-card path, see note below) | ~2s | **3,992ms** ❌ | **5,445ms** ❌ |\n| **End-to-end: utterance end → first partial card** | **< 3s** | **5,219-5,571ms** ❌ | **8,853-10,404ms** ❌ |\n\n**Internal consistency — the e2e row is the binding gate.** The hot-path stages are strictly sequential (STT → router → fetch-window close → synthesize first token → SSE emit), so their targets must sum inside the end-to-end budget: 300 + 500 + 1000 + 1000 + 100 ms ≈ 2.9s against the 3s gate, leaving ~100ms of unallocated slack. (The fetch first-source and escalate rows sit off this critical path — the former lands inside the fetch window, the latter is off the first-card path per the note below.) Synthesize-first-token was originally sketched at ~1–2s — the Hot Path diagram's ~1–2s figure survives as the estimate for the *full* synthesize call, first token through completion — but at a 2s first token the stages sum to ~3.9s: every stage could pass its own budget individually while the end-to-end gate fails, a contradiction that would otherwise surface only at rehearsal. Hence the ~1s first-token allocation above. All per-stage targets remain ESTIMATES and are **provisional allocations, not independent pass/fail gates**: once the Measured p50/p95 columns fill, re-derive the stage allocations from measured slack (a stage running under budget donates headroom to one running over) with the e2e row held fixed and overriding — the Gate below then applies against the re-derived allocations. A run where every stage meets its allocation but the e2e row misses is still a failed run.\n\n**Task-profile first card.** The escalate stage does not gate the <3s promise: for a task-profile thread, the ack/pending card (a served built-in template, not a component-library render — see Component Library → Built-in cards) renders as soon as the router resolves the thread (router + SSE emit only) — before bead formulation and `bf create` complete — then updates in place with the bead ref when the escalate stage returns. The escalate row above budgets that in-place update, not the first card; the end-to-end row applies to the ack card.\n\n**Instrumentation requirement.** The server records per-stage timings for every dispatch — `router_ms`, `fetch_first_source_ms`, `fetch_total_ms` (fetch-window close), `synthesize_first_token_ms`, `synthesize_total_ms`, `escalate_ms` (task-profile dispatches: formulation + validation + `bf create`), `sse_emit_ms`, plus client-reported STT and first-render timestamps when available — and persists them to the session store's `dispatch_timings` table (schema in Data Model). This is not optional telemetry: it is the only way the Measured columns get filled, and it doubles as the per-step timing log the Phase 5 rehearsal checklist requires.\n\n**Gate.** The demo cannot be scheduled until the Measured p50/p95 columns are filled from real runs (rehearsal timing logs count). If measured p95 blows a stage's budget, either the stage gets fixed or the on-screen promise changes — the recording must not showcase a number the system doesn't hit.\n\n### The Async Path\n\n```\nTask-profile intent (research, coding, long-running work)\n  → Generated-Bead Safety validation + approval (see Escalate Strand)\n  → NEEDLE task bead created (bf create)\n  → Existing Claude Code workers pick up bead normally\n  → Bead watcher detects closure event\n  → Result written to session store results table\n  → SSE push to active canvas (or Telegram if no canvas)\n```\n\n> ⚠ The \"or Telegram if no canvas\" leg is NON-FUNCTIONAL today — Telegram delivery has never worked in any deployment (stubbed methods, unreachable bridge URL; see ADR-1). Until ADR-1 lands, a result arriving with no active canvas is persisted to the session store but reaches no surface.\n\nThe diagram above is the happy path only. Beads also get refused, fail repeatedly, or sit unclaimed — and NEEDLE has no built-in circuit breaker: it re-dispatches a failing bead forever. The async path therefore also specifies:\n\n- **Re-dispatch circuit breaker.** NEEDLE exposes no dispatch-attempt API, so the breaker's signal is the bead's own comment stream: a worker that declines a bead appends a structured refusal comment — `bf comment <id> \"REFUSED: <reason>\"` — the same convention the live incident's workers followed when they committed refusal records to the bead. Each tick the watcher checks its open tracked beads (every unresolved `intents.bead_ref`) alongside the closed list, parses comments past a per-bead high-water mark, and persists the running refusal count in the watcher-owned `bead_watch` table (see Data Model) — on disk, so breaker state survives watcher restarts. After N refusals (default 3) or T hours without progress (default 24h — no closure and no new non-refusal comment), it fences the bead — `bf update` to `status=blocked`, so workers stop picking it up — sets the intent's status to `stuck`, and pushes a \"task stuck — needs your input\" card to the active surface with the most recent refusal reason attached.\n- **Terminal failures.** A bead closed as failed/refused resolves its intent to `failed`; the reason surfaces as a card, never silently dropped. User cancellation still maps to `cancelled`.\n- **Visible aging.** Pending cards show elapsed time; a card past its SLA is flagged on canvas before the breaker trips, so a stuck task is never invisible. The SLA is defined, not vibes: built-in per-intent-type defaults (task-profile: 6h; hot-path intents flag at 30s pending, since their budget is 3s) with a per-project `sla_hours` override in the registry entry. Flag ownership is split by path: for bead-backed (task-profile) intents the watcher computes `sla_deadline` at bead creation, stores it in `bead_watch`, and stamps `sla_flagged_at` when the flag fires. Hot-path intents have no bead and no watcher row — their 30s flag is owned by the **canvas client**: a pure client-side timer from the local pending placeholder's creation at submit time (the canvas creates the placeholder before any server response — see Escalate Strand, Pending/ack card render path) applies the aged-pending treatment with no server round-trip, deliberately, because a hung or wedged server is exactly the failure this flag must survive (see the matching Degraded-State UX row).\n\n### The Self-Improvement Path\n\n```\nFeedback signal (explicit instruction or implicit engagement pattern)\n  → Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE task bead)\n      reads target artifact (prompt file, registry YAML)\n      generates update\n      surfaces diff to user\n  → User approves → artifact written → hot-reloaded on next invocation\n```\n","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1213}}}
